chapter_title.ar: باب مَا أُحِلَّ لِلنَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مِنَ النِّسَاءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 54 – What was Permissible for the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> from the women

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/124)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/124)
text: تَزَوَّجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أُمَّ سَلَمَةَ زَوَّجَهَا إِيَّاهُ عُمَرُ بْنُ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ وَ هُوَ صَغِيرٌ لَمْ يَبْلُغِ الْحُلُمَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Salma Bin Al Khattab, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Yaqteen, from Aasim Bin Humeyd, from Ibrahim Bin Abu Yahya,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> married Umm Salma<sup>as</sup>. Umar Bin Abu Salma married her<sup>as</sup> to him<sup>saww</sup>, and he was young and had yet to attain puberty’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘ Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘The Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, did not marry a second wife in Khadijah’s lifetime.’”
